title: Priority Records

infobox.value: * [Bryan Turner](/wiki/Bryan_Turner_(businessman) "Bryan Turner (businessman)") * Mark Cerami * Steve Drath
6430 Sunset Boulevard, [Los Angeles, California](/wiki/Los_Angeles,_California "Los Angeles, California"), U.S.

is an American [distribution company](/wiki/Distribution_company "Distribution company") and [record label](/wiki/Record_label "Record label")[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) known for artists including [N.W.A](/wiki/N.W.A "N.W.A"), [Ice Cube](/wiki/Ice_Cube "Ice Cube"), [MC Ren](/wiki/MC_Ren "MC Ren"), [Dr. Dre](/wiki/Dr._Dre "Dr. Dre"), [Master P](/wiki/Master_P "Master P"), [Snoop Dogg](/wiki/Snoop_Dogg "Snoop Dogg"), [Mos Def](/wiki/Yasiin_Bey "Yasiin Bey"), [Silkk the Shocker](/wiki/Silkk_the_Shocker "Silkk the Shocker"), [Jay-Z](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z"), [Paris](/wiki/Paris_(rapper) "Paris (rapper)"), [Mack 10](/wiki/Mack_10 "Mack 10"), [504 Boyz](/wiki/504_Boyz "504 Boyz"), [Brotha Lynch Hung](/wiki/Brotha_Lynch_Hung "Brotha Lynch Hung"), [C-Murder](/wiki/C-Murder "C-Murder"), [Mia X](/wiki/Mia_X "Mia X"), [Westside Connection](/wiki/Westside_Connection "Westside Connection"), and [Ice-T](/wiki/Ice-T "Ice-T").[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1) It also distributed hip-hop record labels including [Death Row Records](/wiki/Death_Row_Records "Death Row Records"),[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4) [Hoo-Bangin' Records](/wiki/Hoo-Bangin%27_Records "Hoo-Bangin' Records"),[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6) [No Limit Records](/wiki/No_Limit_Records "No Limit Records"),[[7]](#cite_note-7) [Posthuman Records](/wiki/Posthuman_Records "Posthuman Records"),[[8]](#cite_note-8) [Rap-A-Lot Records](/wiki/Rap-A-Lot_Records "Rap-A-Lot Records"),[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10) [Rawkus Records](/wiki/Rawkus_Records "Rawkus Records"),[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12) [Roc-A-Fella Records](/wiki/Roc-A-Fella_Records "Roc-A-Fella Records"),[[13]](#cite_note-13) [Ruthless Records](/wiki/Ruthless_Records "Ruthless Records")[[14]](#cite_note-14)[[15]](#cite_note-:1-15) [Duck Down Records](/wiki/Duck_Down_Records "Duck Down Records"), and [Wu-Tang Records](/wiki/Wu-Tang_Records "Wu-Tang Records").[[16]](#cite_note-16) According to *[Billboard](/wiki/Billboard_(magazine) "Billboard (magazine)")*, "few record labels were as important to the rise of [West Coast hip-hop](/wiki/West_Coast_hip-hop "West Coast hip-hop") as Priority Records".[[17]](#cite_note-billboard2017-17)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American record label

Record label

| Priority Records | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Parent company | [Universal Music Group](/wiki/Universal_Music_Group "Universal Music Group") |
| Founded | 1985; 41 years ago (1985) |
| Founder | * [Bryan Turner](/wiki/Bryan_Turner_(businessman) "Bryan Turner (businessman)") * Mark Cerami * Steve Drath |
| Status | Active |
| Distributor | [Interscope Capitol Labels Group](/wiki/Interscope_Capitol_Labels_Group "Interscope Capitol Labels Group") |
| Genre | Various, with a focus on [hip-hop](/wiki/Hip-hop "Hip-hop") |
| Country of origin | United States |
| Location | 6430 Sunset Boulevard, [Los Angeles, California](/wiki/Los_Angeles,_California "Los Angeles, California"), U.S. |
| Official website | [priorityrecords.com](http://priorityrecords.com) |

**Priority Records** is an American [distribution company](/wiki/Distribution_company "Distribution company") and [record label](/wiki/Record_label "Record label")[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) known for artists including [N.W.A](/wiki/N.W.A "N.W.A"), [Ice Cube](/wiki/Ice_Cube "Ice Cube"), [MC Ren](/wiki/MC_Ren "MC Ren"), [Dr. Dre](/wiki/Dr._Dre "Dr. Dre"), [Master P](/wiki/Master_P "Master P"), [Snoop Dogg](/wiki/Snoop_Dogg "Snoop Dogg"), [Mos Def](/wiki/Yasiin_Bey "Yasiin Bey"), [Silkk the Shocker](/wiki/Silkk_the_Shocker "Silkk the Shocker"), [Jay-Z](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z"), [Paris](/wiki/Paris_(rapper) "Paris (rapper)"), [Mack 10](/wiki/Mack_10 "Mack 10"), [504 Boyz](/wiki/504_Boyz "504 Boyz"), [Brotha Lynch Hung](/wiki/Brotha_Lynch_Hung "Brotha Lynch Hung"), [C-Murder](/wiki/C-Murder "C-Murder"), [Mia X](/wiki/Mia_X "Mia X"), [Westside Connection](/wiki/Westside_Connection "Westside Connection"), and [Ice-T](/wiki/Ice-T "Ice-T").[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1) It also distributed hip-hop record labels including [Death Row Records](/wiki/Death_Row_Records "Death Row Records"),[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4) [Hoo-Bangin' Records](/wiki/Hoo-Bangin%27_Records "Hoo-Bangin' Records"),[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6) [No Limit Records](/wiki/No_Limit_Records "No Limit Records"),[[7]](#cite_note-7) [Posthuman Records](/wiki/Posthuman_Records "Posthuman Records"),[[8]](#cite_note-8) [Rap-A-Lot Records](/wiki/Rap-A-Lot_Records "Rap-A-Lot Records"),[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10) [Rawkus Records](/wiki/Rawkus_Records "Rawkus Records"),[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12) [Roc-A-Fella Records](/wiki/Roc-A-Fella_Records "Roc-A-Fella Records"),[[13]](#cite_note-13) [Ruthless Records](/wiki/Ruthless_Records "Ruthless Records")[[14]](#cite_note-14)[[15]](#cite_note-:1-15) [Duck Down Records](/wiki/Duck_Down_Records "Duck Down Records"), and [Wu-Tang Records](/wiki/Wu-Tang_Records "Wu-Tang Records").[[16]](#cite_note-16) According to *[Billboard](/wiki/Billboard_(magazine) "Billboard (magazine)")*, "few record labels were as important to the rise of [West Coast hip-hop](/wiki/West_Coast_hip-hop "West Coast hip-hop") as Priority Records".[[17]](#cite_note-billboard2017-17)

## Company history

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Priority_Records&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Company history")]

### Beginnings (1985–1996)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Priority_Records&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Beginnings (1985–1996)")]

The Los Angeles-based company (with no ties or relations to a previous Priority Records label that was a subsidiary of what was then [CBS Records](/wiki/Sony_Music "Sony Music")) was formed in 1985 by three former [K-tel](/wiki/K-tel "K-tel") executives: [Bryan Turner](/wiki/Bryan_Turner_(businessman) "Bryan Turner (businessman)"), Mark Cerami and Steve Drath.[[18]](#cite_note-18)[[19]](#cite_note-19) Initial funding was provided by R-tek,[[20]](#cite_note-20) a company headed by former K-tel board members: Ray and Harold Kives, and their company took an initial 50% ownership in Priority. Priority bought out the R-tek interest in 1987.

Its first success came with novelty act [the California Raisins](/wiki/The_California_Raisins "The California Raisins").[[15]](#cite_note-:1-15)[[21]](#cite_note-barker-21) To support early operations, Priority Records licensed repertoire from others and released compilation albums using archive recordings while developing its own artist roster.[[22]](#cite_note-22) Priority Records achieved success as an independent label by developing a precedent-setting "street-based" formula of underground marketing which bypassed mainstream radio.[[23]](#cite_note-23)[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] This approach allowed Priority to sell millions of records without entering into the fiercely competitive major label battles over radio airplay.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] Priority's strategic marketing team was developed by Vice President Alyssa Pisano, who led Priority's Marketing and Creative Services Department between 1987 and 1996. Priority's roster featured numerous gold, platinum and multi-platinum artists, including [N.W.A](/wiki/N.W.A "N.W.A"), [Ice Cube](/wiki/Ice_Cube "Ice Cube"), [MC Ren](/wiki/MC_Ren "MC Ren"), [Eazy-E](/wiki/Eazy-E "Eazy-E"), [Master P](/wiki/Master_P "Master P"), [Snoop Dogg](/wiki/Snoop_Dogg "Snoop Dogg"), [Silkk the Shocker](/wiki/Silkk_the_Shocker "Silkk the Shocker"), [Jay-Z](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z"), [Paris](/wiki/Paris_(rapper) "Paris (rapper)"), [Mack 10](/wiki/Mack_10 "Mack 10"), [504 Boyz](/wiki/504_Boyz "504 Boyz"), [Brotha Lynch Hung](/wiki/Brotha_Lynch_Hung "Brotha Lynch Hung") [C-Murder](/wiki/C-Murder "C-Murder"), [Mia X](/wiki/Mia_X "Mia X"), [Westside Connection](/wiki/Westside_Connection "Westside Connection"), [No Limit](/wiki/No_Limit_Records "No Limit Records") and [Ice-T](/wiki/Ice-T "Ice-T").

Priority distributed rapper [Jay-Z](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z")'s debut album, *[Reasonable Doubt](/wiki/Reasonable_Doubt_(album) "Reasonable Doubt (album)")*, in June 1996 with Jay-Z's company [Roc-A-Fella Records](/wiki/Roc-A-Fella_Records "Roc-A-Fella Records"). The album was a critical success, but a minor success upon release, selling 43,000 copies in its first week and 420,000 copies altogether.[[24]](#cite_note-Stopthebreaks.com-24)[[25]](#cite_note-XXLnumbers-25) It was ultimately certified Platinum by the RIAA. *Reasonable Doubt* was Roc-A-Fella and Jay-Z's only album with Priority Records.

### EMI acquisition (1996–2004)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Priority_Records&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: EMI acquisition (1996–2004)")]

In the early 1990s, the label struck up a distribution deal with [EMI](/wiki/EMI "EMI"), while continuing to operate independently. EMI bought a 50% stake in Priority in 1996, and the remainder in 1998.

In 1997, Priority released the [I'm Bout It soundtrack](/wiki/I%27m_Bout_It_(soundtrack) "I'm Bout It (soundtrack)") by [No Limit Records](/wiki/No_Limit_Records "No Limit Records"). The album was a critical and commercial success, debuting and peaking in the top 5 on the *Billboard 200*. It sold 300,000 copies in its first week and was certified platinum by the RIAA. It featured the hit single *Pushin' Inside You* by Priority/No Limit act [Sons of Funk](/wiki/Sons_of_Funk "Sons of Funk").

In June 1998, Priority and No Limit released Master P's seventh studio album, *[MP Da Last Don](/wiki/MP_Da_Last_Don "MP Da Last Don")*. Promoted as Master P's final album and heavily anticipated, it sold over 495,000 copies in its first week, debuting atop the Billboard 200. The album featured guest appearances from Priority/No Limit artists Snoop Dogg, Sons of Funk, and Silkk the Shocker. *MP Da Last Don* went on to sell over 4.5 million copies in America, being certified quadruple platinum by the RIAA. That October, Priority released [Mack 10](/wiki/Mack_10 "Mack 10")'s third studio album, *[The Recipe](/wiki/The_Recipe_(album) "The Recipe (album)")*. A top 15 charting album on the U.S. *Billboard 200*, the album went on to be certified Gold by the RIAA. *The Recipe* featured guest appearances from Priority artists Master P and Snoop Dogg.

In January 1999, Priority and No Limit released Silkk the Shocker's album, *[Made Man](/wiki/Made_Man_(album) "Made Man (album)")*, which debuted and peaked at No. 1 on the U.S. Billboard 200. The album was certified Platinum by the RIAA.

Despite EMI's full ownership, Priority continued to be an independently managed company until 2001, when its operations were merged into EMI's major U.S. subsidiary, [Capitol Records](/wiki/Capitol_Records "Capitol Records"). Priority was absorbed into Capitol Records in 2004 and ceased operations.[[26]](#cite_note-26)[[17]](#cite_note-billboard2017-17)

### Relaunch (2006–2013)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Priority_Records&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Relaunch (2006–2013)")]

In late 2006, EMI revived Priority Records, and it was supposed to start releasing new records again but never did except for a couple compilation and greatest hits albums from the likes of N.W.A, Westside Connection, Mack 10, and Ice Cube. In 2009, Snoop Dogg was appointed creative chairman of the label; Priority released Snoop Dogg's tenth studio album, *[Malice N Wonderland](/wiki/Malice_N_Wonderland "Malice N Wonderland")* on December 8, 2009.[[27]](#cite_note-27) In July 2013, Priority was re-launched via a joint venture between [Capitol Records](/wiki/Capitol_Records "Capitol Records") and Insurgency Music. Based at the [Capitol Records Building](/wiki/Capitol_Records_Building "Capitol Records Building") in [Los Angeles](/wiki/Los_Angeles "Los Angeles"), the new Priority Records was a producer centric label that focused on a broader array of music genres, including electronic music as well as urban music.[[21]](#cite_note-barker-21)[[28]](#cite_note-28) The lone release under Priority's first relaunch was Snoop Dogg's [Malice n Wonderland](/wiki/Malice_n_Wonderland "Malice n Wonderland"), which was a commercial failure. Following this, Priority remained dormant.

### Second relaunch (2015–present)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Priority_Records&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Second relaunch (2015–present)")]

In 2015, due to the success of the 2015 American [biographical film](/wiki/Biographical_film "Biographical film") *[Straight Outta Compton](/wiki/Straight_Outta_Compton_(film) "Straight Outta Compton (film)")* based on former Priority act [N.W.A](/wiki/N.W.A "N.W.A"), Priority Records was relaunched one more time as a distributor. This time the focus was on new acts, including G Perico, [Snoh Aalegra](/wiki/Snoh_Aalegra "Snoh Aalegra") and Jonn Hart.[[17]](#cite_note-billboard2017-17) In 2018, Priority Records partnered with TuneGO.[[29]](#cite_note-29) Priority Records signed TuneGO Artist, [Three Guests](/wiki/Three_Guests "Three Guests") and distributed their album, *3G*. Priority also signed Massachusetts rap group Bandits The Label and later went on to distribute DtheFlyest debut project "Dope Boy Diaries" which was executive produced by CruufromtheNorth and featured a hit single with rap superstar Lil Baby titled "Fugazi" (2018).[[30]](#cite_note-30)[[31]](#cite_note-31)

## Artists

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Priority_Records&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Artists")]

* [Former Priority Records artists](/wiki/List_of_former_Priority_Records_artists "List of former Priority Records artists")

## Associated labels

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Priority_Records&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Associated labels")]

* [Hoo-Bangin' Records](/wiki/Hoo-Bangin%27_Records "Hoo-Bangin' Records"), founded by [Mack 10](/wiki/Mack_10 "Mack 10")
* [No Limit Records](/wiki/No_Limit_Records "No Limit Records"), founded by [Master P](/wiki/Master_P "Master P"). The label's roster included Master P and [Silkk the Shocker](/wiki/Silkk_the_Shocker "Silkk the Shocker").
* [Roc-A-Fella Records](/wiki/Roc-A-Fella_Records "Roc-A-Fella Records"), founded by [Kareem Burke](/wiki/Kareem_Burke "Kareem Burke"), [Damon Dash](/wiki/Damon_Dash "Damon Dash"), and [Jay-Z](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z"). Roc-A-Fella's lone release with Priority was Jay-Z's 1996 debut album, *[Reasonable Doubt](/wiki/Reasonable_Doubt_(album) "Reasonable Doubt (album)")*.
* [Ruthless Records](/wiki/Ruthless_Records "Ruthless Records").
